commands.repair_reason: No narrow automation repair is indicated; maintainers need to review the intentional TUI route-inheritance behavior and transcript mirror integration before merge.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the diff changes local Gateway delivery and transcript handling without new dependencies, workflow changes, broader permissions, or secret handling. commands.review_summary:
The PR makes TUI and WebChat internal source surfaces, broadcasts message-tool source replies over Gateway chat events, preserves source-reply metadata through media/TTS normalization, updates transcript mirrors, and fixes the TUI client-identity docs.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main shows the WebChat-only internal-source route guard and no source-reply chat final broadcast, and the PR body supplies before-failing and after-fix live TUI proof; I did not rerun it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: No narrow automation repair is indicated; maintainers need to review the intentional TUI route-inheritance behavior and transcript mirror integration before merge.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: TUI-originated chat runs could miss message-tool source replies because the Gateway treated only WebChat as an internal source surface.
- Solution: Treat TUI/WebChat as internal source surfaces, broadcast message-tool source replies back over Gateway chat events, and keep transcript mirrors aligned.
- What changed: Preserved source-reply metadata through reply normalization/media handling/TTS, added source-reply broadcast and transcript mirror upgrades, and kept sensitive source media live-only.
- What did NOT change: External channel delivery policy, provider auth, and normal Pi assistant-turn persistence.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TUI users should see the same visible source reply that the agent sends through the message tool. The previous route/source-surface split let the reply exist in other surfaces or transcript machinery while the active TUI chat stream saw no final reply.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `chat.send` source replies no longer disappear from the active TUI when an agent uses the message tool for the visible source reply.
Real environment tested: Live local dev host with the managed systemd gateway stopped and a foreground gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`. I first ran the same real terminal TUI source-reply scenario on the exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`, then reran it on exact PR head `2716dad60b7e38fc9181ac775998bd70503b9c1c`. Both runs used the configured Codex harness with `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and the same local dev auth state.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway.service
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact
node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-proof-20260519-1312 --message 'Live proof request OPENCLAW_TUI_LIVE_PROOF_20260519_1312. You are in an OpenClaw TUI source conversation. Use the message tool with action="send" and no explicit target or channel to send exactly OPENCLAW_TUI_LIVE_PROOF_20260519_1312 to the current source conversation. After that tool succeeds, reply only NO_REPLY.' --timeout-ms 180000
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call chat.history --json --params '{"sessionKey":"agent:main:live-proof-20260519-1312","limit":20}'
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call sessions.list --json --params '{"limit":1}'
systemctl --user start openclaw-gateway.service
```
Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the live TUI showed the auto-submitted prompt and then rendered `OPENCLAW_TUI_LIVE_PROOF_20260519_1312` in the active TUI chat. `chat.history` for `agent:main:live-proof-20260519-1312` showed the model emitted a `message` tool call with `{"action":"send","message":"OPENCLAW_TUI_LIVE_PROOF_20260519_1312"}`; the tool result reported `"deliveryStatus":"sent"`, `"sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"`, and `"sourceReplySink":"internal-ui"`; the durable mirror assistant message used idempotency key `95756165-642a-42ab-a05c-123cb50ff3dd:internal-source-reply:0`. `sessions.list` reported `status=done`, `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `runtimeMs=13292`, `hasActiveRun=false`.
Observed result after fix: The exact source reply sent through the live `message` tool appeared in the real TUI session and the run returned to idle, proving the fixed Gateway source-reply event path reaches TUI instead of only transcript or another UI surface.
What was not tested: Live external-channel delivery such as Telegram/Discord/Slack was not re-tested for this PR-head proof; this proof targets the reported TUI source-reply route. The existing focused tests cover external route non-inheritance, media mirrors, and sensitive-media handling.
Before evidence: On exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`, I started a foreground base gateway with `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact`, launched `node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-negative-20260519-1331 --message 'Live negative proof request OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331...' --timeout-ms 180000`, then manually submitted the same prompt in the real TUI PTY because base `--message` did not visibly dispatch before connect. The live base TUI returned to `connected | idle` after the run and still showed only the submitted prompt, with no standalone `OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331` reply. Gateway history proved the tool did run: the model emitted `message` with `{"action":"send","message":"OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331"}`, the tool result reported `"deliveryStatus":"sent"`, `"sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"`, and `"sourceReplySink":"internal-ui"`, and a durable `delivery-mirror` assistant message was persisted with idempotency key `0bfe34e8-42ad-465e-a997-3a77d3dd53bc:internal-source-reply:0`. `sessions.list` reported `status=done`, `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `runtimeMs=12079`, `hasActiveRun=false`. That reproduces the reported failure shape: the source reply is sent/persisted, but the active TUI does not receive the visible reply before this patch.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Gateway route resolution and source-reply replay treated WebChat as the internal source surface but did not include the TUI client identity (`openclaw-tui`, mode `ui`).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No Gateway/TUI WebSocket test asserted that message-tool source replies are delivered to TUI clients.
- Contributing context: Source-reply metadata was lost by several normalization/media/TTS transformations, so rich/media source replies could not be reliably mirrored or replayed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` and `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a TUI WebSocket client receives a final chat event for a message-tool internal source reply, including media and sensitive-media mirror handling.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug sits at the Gateway/client event boundary, not inside an external provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

TUI users now see message-tool source replies in the active chat stream. Sensitive media in source replies remains live-only and is not prewritten into durable transcript mirrors.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> no TUI chat final event

After:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> Gateway source-reply final event -> TUI sees reply
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2 via repository wrappers
- Model/provider: live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` for the terminal TUI proof; mocked agent dispatch for focused regression tests
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway TUI/WebSocket client path
- Relevant config (redacted): default local dev config; secrets were not printed

### Steps

1. Applied the TUI source-reply regression test to `origin/main`; it failed with `missing TUI source reply; seen=[]`.
2. Ran the live negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`; the TUI prompt submitted and the model used the message tool, but the active TUI did not render the standalone source reply.
3. Ran the same focused test after this patch; it passed and observed a TUI final event for the internal source reply.
4. Stopped the existing managed gateway, started a foreground PR-head gateway from this worktree on port `18789`, and launched a real terminal TUI with `--session live-proof-20260519-1312` and an auto-submitted proof prompt.
5. Verified the TUI rendered `OPENCLAW_TUI_LIVE_PROOF_20260519_1312` after the live agent used the `message` tool.
6. Queried `chat.history` and `sessions.list` for both base and PR-head runs to confirm the live tool calls, internal source sink results, delivery mirrors, model/provider, and completed run state.
7. Ran the focused dispatch/Gateway/media checks listed below.

### Expected

TUI receives a visible final chat event for the message-tool internal source reply, while durable transcript mirrors avoid raw `MEDIA:` paths and sensitive data URLs.

### Actual

The live base TUI did not render `OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331` even though history showed the `message` tool sent that same text with `sourceReplySink=internal-ui` and persisted a delivery mirror. The live PR-head TUI rendered `OPENCLAW_TUI_LIVE_PROOF_20260519_1312`; history showed the `message` tool sent that same text with `sourceReplySink=internal-ui`; and the regression tests passed for the Gateway/TUI source-reply event path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing live before + passing live after
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: live base failure where message-tool source reply was sent/persisted but not rendered in the active TUI, live terminal TUI source-reply delivery on exact PR head, live Codex harness provider invocation through `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, message-tool internal source sink delivery, TUI source-reply Gateway event delivery, source-reply media mirror upgrade, sensitive source media mirror suppression, metadata preservation through normalization/media/TTS, and route non-inheritance for TUI/WebChat source surfaces.
- Edge cases checked: image-only source replies, delayed source mirrors, dropped media, TTS synthesis, staged audio trust, and sensitive data-image replies.
- What you did **not** verify: live external messaging channel delivery such as Telegram/Discord/Slack.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The source-reply path now creates an additional Gateway final event for internal UI surfaces.
  - Mitigation: Uses distinct source-reply run ids and tests ensure normal external route behavior is unchanged.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "delivers message-tool source replies to TUI clients over gateway chat events"`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-test-src-tui-proof7.tsbuildinfo --pretty false`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/web/tui.md src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/utils/message-channel.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean: no discrete correctness issues found)
